clearpay: Clearpay is the brand name used for Afterpay in the United Kingdom. Same product and technology as Afterpay, just different branding for the UK market. In data, UK transactions may appear under Clearpay branding but use same underlying systems and tables.
bnpl: Buy Now Pay Later - Afterpay's core product allowing consumers to split purchases into interest-free installments. Consumers pay 1/4 upfront and the remaining in 3 installments over 6 weeks. Available in multiple countries (US, AU, NZ, UK, CA).
pi4: Pay-in-4 - Afterpay's flagship BNPL product. Consumers pay 25% upfront and split remaining balance into 3 equal installments paid every 2 weeks. No interest charged if paid on time. Also called Pay-in-4.
pay_in_4: Pay-in-4 - Same as Pi4. Afterpay's core installment payment product splitting purchases into 4 equal payments over 6 weeks.
order: A purchase transaction made by a consumer at a merchant using Afterpay. Primary unit of analysis. Found in AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_ORDER and AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.CUR_C_M_ORDER_MASTER tables.
order_id: Unique identifier for an Afterpay order. Primary key for order-level analysis. Use to join order, installment, and payment tables.
consumer: A customer who uses Afterpay to make purchases. Identified by consumer_id or consumer_uuid. Found in AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.D_CONSUMER.
consumer_id: Unique numeric identifier for an Afterpay consumer. Primary key for consumer-level analysis.
consumer_uuid: Universal unique identifier for an Afterpay consumer. Alternative to consumer_id, used in some tables for cross-system tracking.
merchant: A business that accepts Afterpay as a payment method. Identified by merchant_id. Found in AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.D_MERCHANT and merchant master tables.
merchant_id: Unique identifier for an Afterpay merchant. Primary key for merchant-level analysis. Use to analyze merchant performance and fees.
installment: One of the 4 scheduled payments a consumer must make for an order. Each order has 4 installments. Found in AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_INSTALMENT table.
instalment: British spelling of installment. Used in Afterpay table names (F_INSTALMENT, F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS). Same as installment.
downpayment: The first of 4 payments (25% of order amount) paid by consumer at time of purchase. Afterpay pays merchant full amount upfront.
gpv: Gross Payment Volume - Total dollar value of orders processed through Afterpay. Key metric for business size. Excludes refunds and chargebacks. Use captured_amount field.
gmv: Gross Merchandise Value - Same as GPV. Total value of goods sold through Afterpay / Clearpay platform.
originations: The sum of principal drawn down (loan amount originated). For BNPL, this equals the order amount. Key metric for credit products.
merchant_revenue: Revenue Afterpay earns from merchants, typically a percentage of order amount (merchant fee). Primary revenue source. Found in gross_merchant_revenue field.
merchant_fee: Percentage of order amount that merchant pays to Afterpay. Varies by merchant, country, and channel (online vs instore). Typically 4-6% for online.
gross_merchant_revenue: Total merchant fees collected before any costs. Found in AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.CUR_M_M_MERCHANT_MASTER_WITH_GROSS_REV.
net_merchant_revenue: Merchant revenue after deducting processing costs and other direct costs.
honeymoon_rate: Discounted merchant fee rate offered to new merchants for a limited period as merchant acquisition incentive. Lower than standard rate.
honeymoon_period: Time period during which new merchants receive discounted merchant fees. Tracked in honeymoon_end_date_online field in merchant master table.
late_fee: Fee charged to consumer when they miss an installment payment. Revenue source for Afterpay. Found in late_fee_collected_utd_local field.
processing_cost: Cost to process consumer payments through payment networks. Deducted from gross profit. Found in AP_CUR_XOOP_G.PAYMENTS.F_TRANSACTION_LEVEL_FEES.
ntl: Net Transaction Loss - Key risk metric. Formula: Overdue + Net Chargeback - Late Fee + ATO GWR. Measures actual loss per order. Found in AP_CUR_RISKBI_G.CURATED_RISK_BI_GREEN.DWM_ORDER_LOSS_TAGGING.
net_transaction_loss: Full name for NTL. Total loss on an order after accounting for overdue amounts, chargebacks, late fees collected, and fraud losses.
overdue: Amount of installment payments not paid by consumer after due date. Component of NTL. Tracked at various stages (30, 60, 90, 180 days past due).
overdue_d180: Overdue amount at 180 days past due. Used for calculating final loss. Field: overdue_d180_local.
chargeback: Disputed transaction reversed by card network. Consumer disputes charge with their bank. Afterpay loses the amount. Component of NTL.
chargeback_net: Chargeback amount minus chargeback wins (disputes won by Afterpay). Net loss from chargebacks. Formula: chargeback_amount - chargeback_win_amount.
ato_gwr: Account Takeover Goodwill Refund - Refunds given to consumers for fraudulent account takeover. Component of NTL loss calculation.
gross_profit: Revenue minus direct costs. Formula: Net revenue - contra revenue - processing costs. Key profitability metric before risk losses.
variable_profit: Gross profit minus variable costs including risk losses. Formula: Gross profit - (overdue + chargeback). Key metric for product profitability. Also called VP.
vp: Variable Profit - Abbreviated form. Gross profit minus risk losses (overdue, chargebacks). Primary profitability metric for Afterpay products.
anm: Annualized Net Margin - Key profitability metric annualized to 365 days. Formula: (Gross Profit - Risk Loss) / Principal * 365 / Weighted Average Life. Measures profit rate.
annualized_net_margin: Full name for ANM. Profit margin annualized to compare products with different repayment periods.
wal: Weighted Average Life - Average time for principal to be repaid, weighted by repayment amounts. Used in ANM calculation. For Pi4, typically ~6 weeks.
nac: New Active Customer - Number of consumers who successfully make their first Afterpay order. Key acquisition metric.
new_active_customer: Full name for NAC. First-time Afterpay users who complete an order. Tracked for cohort analysis.
conversion: Rate at which consumers complete checkout and place orders. Key funnel metric. Tracked from checkout start to order completion.
checkout: Process where consumer selects Afterpay as payment method and completes order. Can be online merchant checkout or Afterpay app checkout.
channel: Where order originates: online (merchant website), instore (physical retail), mobile (Afterpay app), portal (Afterpay website). Found in sales_channel field.
online: Orders placed through merchant websites using Afterpay checkout. Largest channel. Filter: sales_channel = 'Online'.
instore: Orders placed at physical retail locations using Afterpay. Uses card or barcode. Filter: sales_channel = 'Instore'.
sales_channel: Field indicating where order was placed: Online, Instore. Use for channel analysis. Found in order master tables.
settlement: When merchant ships order and Afterpay pays merchant. Triggers merchant revenue charge. Can have multiple settlements per order (partial shipments).
captured_amount: Amount actually captured/settled for an order. Equals GPV. Found in captured_amount field in merchant revenue tables.
merchant_revenue_charge_date: Date when Afterpay charges merchant fee. Occurs at settlement when merchant ships order. Use for revenue timing analysis.
order_date: Date when consumer placed the order. Use for cohort analysis and time-based filtering. Standard date field in order tables.
order_datetime: Timestamp when order was created. More precise than order_date. Use for detailed timing analysis.
order_amount: Total purchase amount of the order. What consumer owes across all 4 installments. Found in order_amount_local or order_amount_usd.
order_amount_local: Order amount in merchant's local currency. Use for country-specific analysis without FX conversion.
order_amount_usd: Order amount converted to USD. Use for cross-country aggregation and reporting.
consumer_currency: Currency used by consumer for the order. May differ from merchant currency in cross-border transactions.
order_currency: Currency in which order is denominated. Typically same as merchant currency.
payment: When consumer pays an installment. Tracked in F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS. Multiple payments per order (4 installments).
payment_id: Identifier for a payment transaction. Note: In some tables (RAW_P_E_PAYMENT_PROCESSED) this actually refers to installment_id.
instalment_id: Unique identifier for a specific installment. Use to track individual installment payments and late fees.
payment_status: Status of payment: PAID, FAILED, PENDING. Use to filter successful payments.
payment_source: How consumer paid: bank account, debit card, credit card. Found in payment_source field.
failed_payment: Payment attempt that did not succeed. Tracked in F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS with event_type = 'FAILED_PAYMENT'. May lead to late fees.
due_date: Date when installment payment is due. Found in original_due_date field. Payments after this date incur late fees.
original_due_date: Scheduled due date for installment payment. Typically 2, 4, and 6 weeks after order date for installments 2-4.
invoiced: When installment is billed to consumer. Event_type = 'INVOICED' in F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS. Occurs at order creation and each installment due date.
refund: Money returned to consumer for returned merchandise. Reduces GPV and revenue. Tracked in total_refunded_amount_local field.
dispute: Consumer challenges a transaction. Can result in chargeback. Tracked for risk analysis.
goodwill: Refund given to consumer for service issues or disputes, even without merchant return. Afterpay absorbs cost. Part of loss calculations.
gift_cards: Afterpay Gift Cards product. Consumers purchase e-gift cards using Pi4 to use at merchants. Available in AU, NZ, US, GB, CA. Also called GC.
gc: Gift Cards - Abbreviated form. Afterpay product allowing Pi4 purchase of gift cards. Filter: giftcard_flag = 'True'.
plus_card: Afterpay Plus Card - Credit card product (Mastercard) allowing consumers to pay in installments anywhere. Membership-based. Available in AU, US.
plus_card_oon: Plus Card Out of Network - Plus Card transactions at merchants not directly integrated with Afterpay. Filter: plus_card_oon_yn = 1.
sup: Single Use Payment - Virtual card number for one-time use at out-of-network merchants. Enables BNPL anywhere. Filter: order_transaction_source = 'single_use_card'.
single_use_card: Same as SUP. Virtual payment card for single transaction at non-Afterpay merchants. Extends BNPL to any merchant.
pay_monthly: Afterpay Pay Monthly - Longer-term installment product (not 6 weeks). Consumer lending product. Filter: payment_type = 'PCL' or is_consumer_lending = 1.
pcl: Pay Consumer Lending - Code for Pay Monthly product. Found in payment_type field. Longer repayment terms than Pi4.
consumer_lending: Lending products including Pay Monthly. Longer-term credit products beyond standard 6-week Pi4. Filter: is_consumer_lending = 1.
retro: Retroactive Financing - Convert existing Cash Card purchase into Afterpay loan after transaction settles. Also called APONCAC (Afterpay on Cash App Card).
aponcac: Afterpay on Cash App Card - Post-purchase financing. Convert Cash Card purchase into 6-week installment loan. US only. Same as Retro.
apcac: Afterpay on Cash App Card - Pre-purchase financing. Activate Afterpay toggle on Cash Card before purchase to automatically split into installments. US only.
cash_app_pay: Cash App Pay - Use Cash App to pay at merchants. Immediate payment (not a loan). Different from Afterpay BNPL. Also called CAP.
cap: Cash App Pay - Abbreviated form. Immediate payment product, not installment-based like Afterpay Pi4.
convergence: Integration of Afterpay and Cash App products. Strategic initiative to link Cash App customers with Afterpay BNPL capabilities.
idv: Identity Verification - Process where consumers verify identity by providing SSN, date of birth, address. Required for higher limits and certain features.
identity_verification: Full name for IDV. KYC process to verify consumer identity. Tracked in onboarding flow. Required for account upgrades.
kyc: Know Your Customer - Compliance process for verifying consumer identity. Part of IDV. Required for regulatory compliance.
registration: When consumer creates Afterpay account. Tracked in D_CONSUMER table and registration.v1 events. First step before making orders.
registration_origin: Where consumer account was created: checkout, mobile app, portal. Use to track acquisition channel.
onboarding: Process of new consumer signing up and completing first order. Tracked in onboarding funnel metrics and Amplitude events.
account_created: When consumer account is created in Afterpay system. Event: production.event.paylater.consumer.registration.v1.
credit_check: Risk assessment when consumer applies or places order. Determines approval and spending limit. Tracked in credit risk tables.
credit_contract_accepted: Consumer accepts credit terms. Event: production.event.paylater.consumer.account.credit-contract-accepted.v1. Required before first order.
spending_limit: Maximum amount consumer can have in outstanding orders. Determined by credit check. Increases with good payment history.
green: Data classification tier for non-PII data. Accessible to broader audience. Databases: AP_RAW_GREEN, AP_CUR_BI_G. Based on Traffic Light Protocol.
red: Data classification tier for PII/sensitive data (DSL3). Restricted access. Databases: AP_RAW_RED_CSMR, AP_RAW_RED_MERCH. Requires special approval.
dsl3: Data Security Level 3 - PII and sensitive data. Classified as 'red' tier. Requires restricted access and special permissions.
ap_raw_green: Snowflake database for raw Afterpay data without PII. Schema: GREEN. Primary source for order, consumer, merchant data. Accessible to most analysts.
ap_cur_bi_g: Snowflake database for curated Afterpay analytics data (green tier). Schema: AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN. Contains dimensional and aggregated tables.
ap_raw_red_csmr: Snowflake database for raw consumer PII data (red tier). Restricted access. Contains DSL3 consumer information.
ap_raw_red_merch: Snowflake database for raw merchant PII data (red tier). Restricted access. Contains DSL3 merchant information.
ap_cur_riskbi_g: Snowflake database for curated risk and loss data (green tier). Schema: CURATED_RISK_BI_GREEN. Contains NTL forecasts and loss tagging.
ap_cur_xoop_g: Snowflake database for payments data (green tier). Schema: PAYMENTS. Contains processing costs and transaction fees.
f_order: Fact table containing all Afterpay orders. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_ORDER. Primary source for order data. Clustered by PAR_REGION.
d_consumer: Dimension table for consumer attributes. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.D_CONSUMER. Contains consumer demographics and account info.
d_merchant: Dimension table for merchant attributes. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.D_MERCHANT. Contains merchant business information.
f_instalment: Fact table for installment details. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_INSTALMENT. One row per installment. Links to F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS for payments.
f_instalment_events: Fact table for installment payment events. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS. Tracks payments, failures, late fees per installment.
f_late_fee_events: Fact table for late fee events. Located at AP_RAW_GREEN.GREEN.F_LATE_FEE_EVENTS. Tracks late fee invoicing and collection.
cur_c_m_order_master: Curated view of order master data. Located at AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.CUR_C_M_ORDER_MASTER. Primary table for order analysis.
dwm_order_master_tbl: Data warehouse model for order master. Located at AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.DWM_ORDER_MASTER_TBL. Source for CUR_C_M_ORDER_MASTER view.
dwm_order_loss_tagging: Table with order-level loss metrics and tagging. Located at AP_CUR_RISKBI_G.CURATED_RISK_BI_GREEN.DWM_ORDER_LOSS_TAGGING. Source for NTL calculations.
dwm_ntl_order_forecast: Table with forecasted NTL per order. Located at AP_CUR_RISKBI_G.CURATED_RISK_BI_GREEN.DWM_NTL_ORDER_FORECAST. Used before actual losses mature.
dwm_merchant_master_tbl: Data warehouse model for merchant master. Located at AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.DWM_MERCHANT_MASTER_TBL. Contains merchant attributes and fees.
cur_m_m_merchant_master_with_gross_rev: Curated merchant master with revenue. Located at AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.CUR_M_M_MERCHANT_MASTER_WITH_GROSS_REV. Links merchants to order revenue.
dwm_ntm_order_margin: Net Transaction Margin table. Located at AP_CUR_BI_G.CURATED_ANALYTICS_GREEN.DWM_NTM_ORDER_MARGIN. Contains order-level profitability metrics.
product_line: High-level product categorization: BNPL_ONLINE, BNPL_INSTORE, PLUS_CARD, GIFTCARD, SUP, PAY_MONTHLY. Use for product analysis.
product_sub: Detailed product classification: 3070-Core Buy Now Pay Later, 3073-Consumer Lending, 3074-Membership, Gift Card, 3070-Core Buy Now Pay Later SUP.
order_transaction_source: Source of order transaction: standard checkout, single_use_card (SUP), anywhere_card (Plus Card). Use to identify product type.
shop_directory_name: Name of shop in Afterpay directory. Value 'afterpay' with single_use_card indicates SUP transaction.
gdp_region: Geographic region for GDPR compliance: AU, US, UK, EU. Use for regional analysis. Found in raw tables.
consumer_country_code: Country code for consumer. Use for geographic segmentation. Common values: AU, US, GB, NZ, CA.
key_country_code: Primary country code field in some tables. Same as consumer_country_code. Use for country-level analysis.
par_region: Partition region for data clustering. Used in raw tables for query optimization. Values: AU, US, UK, EU.
anz: Australia and New Zealand region. Used for regional analysis. Countries: AU, NZ.
na: North America region. Used for regional analysis. Countries: US, CA.
amplitude: Analytics platform for tracking consumer events. Project: Afterpay Health and Lending. Use for behavioral analysis and funnel tracking.
event_properties_category: Amplitude field indicating which surface event was emitted from: Web Browser, Mobile App, Checkout, Portal.
platform: Platform where event occurred: Web (checkout/portal), iOS, Android. Use to segment by device type.
ddf: Data Development Framework - Afterpay's data pipeline framework. Used for creating curated tables and data products.
robot_user: Service account for automated data pipelines. Examples: ap-cur-r-featsci, ap-cur-r-riskmdl. Used for bot access to databases.
bot_user: Same as robot_user. Automated service account for data access. Managed through Registry for permissions.
registry: Block's access management system. Use to request database access and manage permissions. URL: registry.sqprod.co.
amundsen: Data discovery platform. Use to search for Afterpay tables and understand data lineage. URL: go/amundsen.
mode: Business intelligence tool for SQL queries and dashboards. Mode collection: Afterpay Health and Lending. Use for ad-hoc analysis.
databricks: Alternative data platform for Afterpay. Databases: AP_RAW_GREEN, AP_CUR_CRRSKBR_G. Used alongside Snowflake.
vega: Legacy Afterpay data platform. Being migrated to Block's Snowflake infrastructure. Historical reference.
orion: Project Orion - Multi-phase initiative to migrate Afterpay data to Block's data platforms. Enables cross-brand data joining.
axon: Project Axon - Data Modeling Framework using dbt for Afterpay. Focused on reusability, reliability, and observability of data pipelines.
uniform_migration: Migration from Delta format to UniForm (Iceberg + Delta) for Afterpay datalake. Optimizes Snowflake persistence.
delta: Data format used in Afterpay datalake. Being enhanced with Iceberg metadata layer through UniForm migration.
iceberg: Open table format being added to Afterpay datalake. Part of UniForm migration for better Snowflake integration.
dbt: Data Build Tool - Used in Project Axon for data modeling and transformations. Integrated into Afterpay's Data Development Framework.
aida: Analytics, Insights & Data Architecture - Team and initiative for Afterpay data engineering. Owns data platform and infrastructure.
kafka: Event streaming platform. Afterpay uses Kafka for real-time data ingestion. Project Stellar automates Kafka ingest workflows.
stellar: Project Stellar - Kafka ingest workflow automation using Karl and Forge. Self-serve data ingestion into Open Datalake.
karl: Workflow automation framework used in Project Stellar. Enables self-serve Kafka data ingestion.
ozone: Deployment framework for running traceable, peer-reviewable operations. Supports Snowflake and Databricks. Reduces operational risk.
interchange_revenue: Revenue from interchange fees on card transactions. Component of total revenue for card-based products like Plus Card.
interest_paid: Interest paid by consumers on lending products like Pay Monthly. Revenue component for longer-term credit products.
contra_revenue: Revenue deductions including issuing costs and origination fees. Subtracted from gross revenue to get net revenue.
feb_origination_cost: Front-End-Book origination cost. Cost to originate loans. Deducted from revenue in ANM calculation.
originated_principal: Principal amount originated (loaned). Used in ANM calculation. For BNPL, equals order amount.
principal_paid: Amount of principal repaid by consumers. Used to calculate weighted average life and ANM.
principal_paid_weighted: Principal repayment weighted by time. Used to calculate weighted average life (WAL) in ANM formula.
90dpd: 90 Days Past Due - Milestone for measuring loan loss. Principal not paid back at 90dpd is considered loss. Key risk metric.
days_past_due: Number of days since payment was due. Used to classify overdue severity: 30dpd, 60dpd, 90dpd, 180dpd.
od_seg: Overdue segment classification. Used to exclude merchandise overdue from loss calculations. Filter: od_seg <> '01.Merchandise Overdue'.
cb_seg: Chargeback segment classification. Used to exclude merchandise chargebacks from loss calculations. Filter: cb_seg <> '0. Merchandise'.
ntl_forecast: Forecasted Net Transaction Loss. Predicted loss for orders before actual losses mature. Found in DWM_NTL_ORDER_FORECAST table.
overdue_forecast: Forecasted overdue amount. Predicted overdue losses before they materialize. Component of ntl_forecast.
chargeback_forecast: Forecasted chargeback amount. Predicted chargeback losses. Component of ntl_forecast.
late_fee_forecast: Forecasted late fee collection. Predicted late fees to be collected. Reduces ntl_forecast.
loss_forecast: General term for predicted losses. Includes overdue_forecast, chargeback_forecast, minus late_fee_forecast.
post_180: After 180 days past due. Used to calculate final losses. Field: post_180_late_fee_local for late fees collected after 180dpd.
merchandise_overdue: Overdue amount related to merchandise disputes/returns. Excluded from NTL calculation. Filter: od_seg = '01.Merchandise Overdue'.
affiliate_revenue: Revenue from affiliate marketing. When consumers discover merchants through Afterpay shop directory. Found in dwm_shop_event_order_attribution.
cpc: Cost Per Click - Pricing model for affiliate/advertising revenue. Merchants pay per click from Afterpay platform.
cpm: Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions) - Pricing model for advertising revenue. Merchants pay per 1000 views.
shop_directory: Afterpay's merchant directory where consumers browse and discover merchants. Generates affiliate revenue.
take_consumer_currency: Afterpay's take (revenue) in consumer's currency. Total revenue from order including merchant fees and late fees.
payment_type: Type of payment product: standard Pi4, PCL (Pay Monthly), etc. Use to filter by product type.
payment_method_id: Identifier for consumer's payment method (bank account, card). Links to payment instrument details.
core_captured_date: Date when order was captured in core system. Use for settlement timing analysis.
event_date: Date when event occurred. Generic date field in various event tables. Use for time-based filtering.
event_datetime: Timestamp when event occurred. More precise than event_date. Found in installment and payment event tables.
event_type: Type of event: INVOICED, PAID, FAILED_PAYMENT, etc. Use to filter specific event types in F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS.
amount_paid: Amount paid in a payment event. Found in F_INSTALMENT_EVENTS and F_LATE_FEE_EVENTS.
original_amount: Original scheduled amount for installment. Found in F_INSTALMENT. May differ from amount_paid if partial payment.
variable_percent: Variable percentage of merchant fee. Found in F_MERCHANT_FEE. Use to calculate merchant revenue.
charge_date: Date when merchant fee was charged. Found in F_MERCHANT_FEE. Use for revenue recognition timing.
billing_period_start: Start date of Plus Card membership billing period. Used to match orders to membership invoices.
billing_period_end: End date of Plus Card membership billing period. Used to match orders to membership invoices.
subscription_revenue: Revenue from Plus Card membership subscriptions. Sum of amount_paid from RAW_C_E_MEMBERSHIP_INVOICE.
anywhere_card: Plus Card used anywhere (out of network). Filter: order_transaction_source = 'ANYWHERE_CARD'.
inn: In Network - Merchants directly integrated with Afterpay. Standard BNPL checkout flow.
oon: Out of Network - Merchants not directly integrated. Accessed via SUP, Plus Card, or Cash App convergence products.
topaz_id: Identifier in legacy payment platform (Pagantis acquisition). Used for EU transaction processing. Not linked to core F_PAYMENT table.
eu_processing: EU countries (Spain, France, Italy) use different processing from Pagantis acquisition. No processing_cost data in standard tables.
pagantis: Spanish BNPL company acquired by Afterpay. Legacy platform for EU transactions. Being migrated to core Afterpay systems.
data_mesh: Data architecture approach used by Afterpay. Domain-oriented, self-serve data infrastructure. See go/DataMesh-Afterpay-Learnings.
traffic_light_protocol: Data classification system: Green (non-PII), Red (PII/DSL3). Based on CISA cybersecurity framework. Used for access control.
aov: Average Order Volume calculates the total gpv divided by the total number of unqiue orders (order_id)
H1: The first half of the calendar year (Jan through June)
H2: the second half of the calendar year (July through December
